statement.en: SENSORY, PHYSICAL, AND FUNCTIONAL PROPERTIES OF PART SKIM, PASTA FILATA MOZZARELLA MADE WITH OR WITHOUT LACTOBACILLUS CASEI ADJUNCT CULTURE
statement.summary: As mozzarella consumption increases in the United States, innovation in formulation has also grown. While studies have focused on novel ingredient implementation, the opportunity to apply adjunct or secondary cultures has been minimally explored in mozzarella. One adjunct culture, Lactobacillus casei, shows promise to increase positive flavors and improve functional properties in baked applications of mozzarella cheese. The overall objective of this study was to understand the influence of L. casei adjunct culture on the sensory, physical, and functional properties of part-skim pasta filata mozzarella. Mozzarella was prepared in two treatments: a control with S. thermophilus, and a modified with S. thermophilus and L. casei. Mozzarella was stored at 3.3ºC, then assessed over storage at day

The core claims about L. casei adjunct culture effects on mozzarella sensory/physical/functional properties are empirically testable but the abstract is incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence). Classification as FLOWING rather than TRUE because: (1) full dataset/results not provided in excerpt; (2) validation pending peer review completion; (3) sensory claims require replication across contexts. If full results support hypotheses, would shift toward TRUE; if null/contradictory, toward FALSE.
